title: Bottom-up structural analysis of amides by identifying collision-induced dissociation fragment ions: An application in bile acid-amino acid conjugates-targeted sub-metabolome profiling.
author_string: Zhou, Yuxuan, Li, Wei, Niu, Xiaoya, Cao, Yan, Wang, Maodong et al.
abstract: Amide serves as the key functional group for several chemical families, such as proteins, peptides, amides, and amino acid-conjugated bile acids (AA-BAs), and the featured fragment ions (e.g., a<sup>+/-</sup>, b<sup>+/-</sup>, c<sup>+/-</sup>, x<sup>+/-</sup>, y<sup>+/-</sup>, and z<sup>+/-</sup>) are usually the determinant clues for MS/MS-dependent structural analysis. Here, efforts were made to identify the fragment ions through matching full exciting energy ramp (FEER)-MS<sup>3</sup> spectra with authentic amines and/or known substructures, and the justified strategy was thereafter applied to profile AA-BAs-targeted sub-metabolome in biological matrices. Forty-six amides were collected to configure FEER-MS<sup>3</sup> for the featured fragment ions, and FEER-MS<sup>2</sup> spectra were acquired for some relevant amines. When amides owned identical substructure, they generated the same FEER-MS<sup>3</sup> spectra correlating to the building blocks, and FEER-MS<sup>3</sup> spectrum of y<sup>+</sup> ion for a given amide matched exactly with FEER-MS<sup>2</sup> of the pioneer amine. To assist structural analysis of AA-BAs, 112 authentic compounds were obtained by integrating commercial collection, synthesis, and biotransformation. Through matching FEER-MS<sup>3</sup> spectra with FEER-MS<sup>3</sup> of known substructures or FEER-MS<sup>2</sup> of authentic amines, 85 AA-BAs were identified from a pooled biological sample. Quantitative sub-metabolome comparison involving all detected AA-BAs was conducted between healthy and CCl<sub>4</sub>-induced liver injury rats. Ala-CA, Leu-CA, Trp-CA, Tyr-CA, Phe-CA, together with Ile-UCA isomer served as the primary differential AA-BA species. Differential AA-BAs bearing confirmative identities were disclosed to discriminate liver injury rats from healthy animals. More importantly, a meaningful bottom-up structural analysis strategy that deciphers the featured fragment ions via matching FEER-MS<sup>n</sup> (n = 2 or 3) with authentic pioneers or known substructures was constructed for amides as well as other chemical families.
